metadata.title:
DO WOMEN WANT TO VOTE?

SHOULD THE WOMEN WANT TO VOTE?

(AFFIRMATIVE)


BY Mrs Harriet Taylor Upton,
Treasurer National American Woman Suffrage Association

pages.raw_text:
believe that it would be a good
thing to have the mother element
represented in the government. The
government is really larger home.
Men never in their lives have made
q home. They have made houses,
but by themselves they never have
made such home that anybody
cared to live in.


To be sure, there have been her
mits and men of peculiar charac-
tiers, who have kept bachelors
hall, but even the bachelors apart
ments Of men of our time are as a
rule directed by a housekeeper, or
overlooked in some way by some
woman. This statement that the
government is but larger home
is so self-apparent that it needs no
argument. When that is admitted,
we have admitted all. We know
that the home is the strength OF
the nation, and everybody knows
that the women are the strength
Of the homes.
